name: revel

metadata.name: revel
metadata.summary: Effortlessly spice up your terminal apps using colors, progressbars & more

documentation:
# Revel

`revel` helps you create beautiful interfaces for your command line tools. Best
of all, it's extremely easy to use.

Simply replace the standard `print` function with revel's and just like that you
can now display colors!

```py
from revel import print

print('[green]Hello, World![/]')
```

## Styles

`revel` uses BBCode-style markup to style your text. A variety of styles
are available:

- Formatting:
  - bold
  - dim
  - italic
  - underlined
  - inverted
  - strikethrough

- Text Colors:
  - black
  - red
  - green
  - blue
  - magenta
  - cyan
  - white

- Background Colors:
  - bg-black
  - bg-red
  - bg-green
  - bg-blue
  - bg-magenta
  - bg-cyan
  - bg-white

Adding `weak` to a tag will only apply the styles of that tag, if no competing styles are already applied. For example:

```py
print("[red]Hello, [weak blue underlined]World![/][/]")
```

This will be displayed entirely in red (since the weak "blue" formatting is
overridden by "red"), but the "underlined" formatting will still be applied.

## Progressbars

Adding progressbars is easy as can be:

```py
from revel import print, ProgressBar
import time

with ProgressBar(max=10, unit="percent") as bar:
    for ii in range(10):
        bar.progress = ii
        time.sleep(1.0)
```

You can switch units between "count", "percent", "byte" and "time". The bar will
also automatically display how much time remains until completion, once it is
confident in its estimate.

## Edit text after it has already been printed

Docked widgets can be edited at will. For example, this example docks a text
line and updates it repeatedly:

```py
from revel import print
import time

line = print("Looking for files", dock=True)

for ii in range(1, 10):
    line.text = f"Found {ii} file(s)"
    time.sleep(1.0)
```

## Additional `print`-like methods

In addition to `print`, revel also provides `success`, `warning`, `error` and
`fatal`. These will be displayed in color, drawing attention to the message.

## TODO

- Implement the `verbatim` tag
- Jupyter Notebook support
- Test on bad terminals
- Actual docs
- Showcase features:
  - `print_chapter`
  - `select`
  - `select_multiple`
  - `select_short`
    - `select_yes_no`
  - `input_key`
  - `input`
    - general improvements, such as parsing, default
    - secret input
  - `escape`
  - `unescape`

functions.name: revel.rapidbaby.common.escape
documentation: Escapes a string to prevent markup from being interpreted.

classes.name: revel.errors.RevelError

methods.name: revel.errors.RevelError.message
documentation: The error message.
Base class for all Revel exceptions.

classes.name: revel.errors.NoOptionGivenError
documentation: Raised when the user doesn't specify a command.

classes.name: revel.errors.NoSuchOptionError
documentation: Raised when the user passes an invalid option.

classes.name: revel.errors.AmbiguousOptionError
documentation: Raised when the user passes an ambiguous option.

classes.name: revel.errors.ArgumentError
documentation:
Raised when attempting to call a function with invalid arguments. The
message is human-readable and meant to be directly passed to the user

methods.name: revel.rapidbaby.styles.StyleSet._add_fundamental_style
documentation: Registers a new style.

methods.name: revel.rapidbaby.styles.StyleSet.add_alias
documentation: Adds a style which is an alias for one or more other styles.

functions.name: revel.argument_parser.parameters_from_function
documentation: Converts the given function's parameter list into a list of `Parameter`s.

functions.name: revel.argument_parser.ask_value_for_parameter
documentation: Ask the user for a value for the given parameter, parse and return it.

functions.name: revel.argument_parser.parse_function_parameters
documentation:
Given a list of parameters, parse the given arguments and return them in a
form usable to call the function. If `interactive` is `True`, ask for any
missing values interactively.

The result is a tuple of args and kwargs, which can be used to call the
target function as `target(*args, **kwargs)`.

Raises:
    ArgumentError: If the arguments are invalid

functions.name: revel.argument_parser.parse_function_name
documentation:
Given a list of functions and their names, extract the name of the function
to call from the given arguments. The function name is expected to be the
first argument, and the remainder are returned.

If no function name was provided, and `interactive` is `True`, ask for the
function name interactively.

Raises:
    NoOptionGivenError: If the user didn't specify a function name and
        `interactive` is `False`.

    NoSuchOptionError: If the user's input doesn't match any of the function
        names and `interactive` is `False`.

    AmbiguousOptionError: If the user's input matches multiple choices and
        `interactive` is `False`.

methods.name: revel.argument_parser.Parser.feed_one
documentation: Process a single value, updating the parser's state accordingly.

methods.name: revel.argument_parser.Parser.feed_many
documentation: Process multiple values, updating the parser's state accordingly.

methods.name: revel.argument_parser.Parser.finish
documentation: Complete the parsing process and return the assigned values.

functions.name: revel.menu.select
documentation:
Asks the user to select one of the given options. If `prompt` is given, it
is displayed first. The options are displayed as a numbered list, and the
user is asked to select one of them.

functions.name: revel.menu.select_multiple
documentation:
Allows the user to choose between a set of options. A menu will be displayed
on the screen with all the options, and the user can select multiple options
by toggling them on and off.

methods.name: revel.menu.Menu.on_input
documentation:
Run arbitrary code to handle the given key. If this returns `True`, the
menu will be closed.

functions.name: revel.common.comma_separated_list
documentation:
Given a list of values, return a human-readable list of them. If the list
contains only one value, that value is returned. Otherwise, the values are
joined with commas, and the conjunction is inserted before the last value.

The list is a comma-separated list of the values, with the conjunction
inserted before the last value. For example, if the conjunction is "or", the
list will be "A, B or C".

If `quote` is not `None`, each value will be surrounded with the quote
character.

functions.name: revel.common.choose_string
documentation:
Given a list of choices and user input, find the choice the user wanted to
select. This is a best-effort method, which does it's best to give the user
leeway in what they type, without accidentally selecting an option they
didn't want to select.

If the function is confident in what the user wanted to select, it returns
the index of the choice. Otherwise, it raises an exception.

## Raises

`NoSuchOptionError`: If the user's input doesn't match any of the choices.

`AmbiguousOptionError`: If the user's input matches multiple choices.

functions.name: revel.common.python_name_to_console
documentation:
Convert a name as it would be used in Python to how it would be used in the
console. For example, `foo_bar` becomes `foo-bar`.

functions.name: revel.common.python_name_to_pretty
documentation: Convert a name as it would be used in Python to a nice, human-readable name.

functions.name: revel.common.multiline_strip
documentation: Clean up a user-provided multi-line string, such as a description.

functions.name: revel.common.an
documentation: Given a noun, return the correct indefinite article to use before it.

methods.name: revel.rapidbaby.parser.Baby.reset
documentation:
Resets the parser to its initial state. Does not modify the known
styles.

methods.name: revel.rapidbaby.parser.Baby.finish
documentation:
Closes all open tags and returns the final result.

This function leaves the parser in an undefined state. Make sure to
`reset` it before using it again.

methods.name: revel.rapidbaby.parser.Baby.process
documentation:
Applies markup to a string and returns the result. This is a convenience
method that combines `reset`, `feed`, and `finish`.

functions.name: revel.markup.unescape
documentation: Applies markup to a string.

functions.name: revel.shell_escape.guess_shell
documentation: Attempt to determine the shell this program is running in.

functions.name: revel.shell_escape.shell_escape
documentation:
Given any value, return a string that represents the same value in a shell.
If the value is not a string it will be converted to a string using `str`.

Warning: This function is not guaranteed to be safe. It is a rather
    simplistic implementation that will _usually_ work.

methods.name: revel.cli_app.App.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new CLI app. The app contains all information to run a CLI
application, including commands, options, and help text.

## Args

`nicename`: A human-readable, nice name for the application. This will
be used in texts to refer to the application.

`command_name`: The name of the command that will be used to run the app
from the terminal.

`summary`: A short description of the application. Should be at most one
line.

`details`: A longer, more detailed description of the application. This
may span multiple paragraphs and will only be displayed when the user
asks for it, such as in the help text.

`version`: The version of the application. If provided, a `version`
command will be added.

`add_help_command`: If `True`, a `help` command will be added to the
app.

methods.name: revel.cli_app.App.command
documentation:
Register a function as a command, allowing users to call the function
from the CLI. The function's name will be used as the command's name.

functions.name: revel.legacy._detect_mode
documentation: Keep track of the environment the script is running in

functions.name: revel.legacy.input_key
documentation:
Get a single keypress from the user.

Regular characters are returned as-is, any special keys (such as "enter")
are translated to strings. The strings for regular characters are always one
character long, while the strings for special keys are guaranteed to be
longer.

In addition to regular characters, the following special keys strings may be
returned:

- escape
- backspace
- up
- down
- left
- right
- insert
- home
- end
- page-up
- page-down
- f1
- f2
- f3
- f4
- f5
- f6
- f7
- f8
- f9
- f10
- f11
- f12
- delete
- ctrl-a
- ctrl-b
- ctrl-c
- ctrl-d
- ctrl-e
- ctrl-f
- ctrl-g
- ctrl-h
- ctrl-i
- ctrl-j
- ctrl-k
- ctrl-l
- ctrl-m
- ctrl-n
- ctrl-o
- ctrl-p
- ctrl-q
- ctrl-r
- ctrl-s
- ctrl-t
- ctrl-u
- ctrl-v
- ctrl-w
- ctrl-x
- ctrl-y
- ctrl-z

Note that not all keys can be detected on all platforms. That's because some
platforms use the same internal code for different keys, making it
impossible to distinguish them.

If `raise_on_eof` is `True`, an `EOFError` will be raised if the user
presses Ctrl+D. If `raise_on_interrupt` is `True`, a `KeyboardInterrupt` is
raised if the user presses Ctrl+C. These are the default, to allow users to
interrupt the program as they would expect.

functions.name: revel.legacy.get_docked_widget
documentation: Returns the currently docked widget, or `None` if no widget is docked.

functions.name: revel.legacy.print
documentation:
Prints a widget to the terminal, optionally docking it.

If a single widget is passed it is returned, otherwise the inputs are
converted to strings, joined with a space and returned as a `TextLine`.

functions.name: revel.legacy.debug
documentation: Displays a debug message, highlighted to draw attention to it.

functions.name: revel.legacy.success
documentation: Displays a success message, highlighted to draw attention to it.

functions.name: revel.legacy.warning
documentation: Displays a warning message, highlighted to draw attention to it.

functions.name: revel.legacy.error
documentation: Displays an error message, highlighted to draw attention to it.

functions.name: revel.legacy.fatal
documentation:
Displays an error message, highlighted to draw attention to it, and then
exits the program.

functions.name: revel.legacy.print_chapter
documentation:
Displays a chapter heading. Chapters are highlighted and create whitespace
to visually separate them from other widgets.

functions.name: revel.legacy._secret_input
documentation: Get input from `sys.stdin`, displaying an asterisk for each character.

functions.name: revel.legacy.input
documentation:
Asks the user for a value and returns it. If `prompt` is given, it is
displayed first. `parse` is applied to the user's input, and its result
returned. If `parse` raises a `ValueError` the user is asked for another
value. Lastly, if `default` is given, it is returned if the user enters an
empty string.

functions.name: revel.legacy.select_short
documentation:
Allows the user to choose between a set of options. The options are expected
to be few and short, as they're styled in a single line.

## Args

`prompt`: The question to ask the user.

`options`: A dictionary mapping the user's input to the corresponding value.
This value will be returned should the user select the option.

`default_str`: The default option, as a string. If the user enters an empty
string, this option is returned. If `None`, there is no default option. The
user will be forced to enter a valid option in this case.

`add_yes_no_options`: If `True` the available options will be padded with
common yes/no options, such as "y", "true", "y", etc.

functions.name: revel.legacy.select_yes_no
documentation:
Displays the prompt to the user, and returns True if the user responds with
yes, and False if the user responds with no. If no input is entered and a
default value is provided it is returned instead.

The function makes a best effort to interpret the user's input, and will
accept a variety of possible values.

methods.name: revel.legacy.Widget.mark_dirty
documentation: Mark this widget as dirty, requesting a redraw.

methods.name: revel.legacy.Widget.is_docked
documentation: Returns `True` if this widget is currently docked and `False` otherwise.

methods.name: revel.legacy.Widget.draw
documentation:
Draw the widget. The curser is at the top left position of the widget.

TODO: Where should the cursor be after the widget is drawn?

classes.name: revel.legacy.TextLine
documentation: A simple line of text, optionally formatted with `rich` style markup.

classes.name: revel.legacy.Unit

methods.name: revel.legacy.Unit._units
documentation:
Return names of all units for this type and the divisor to convert from
the base unit to them. The results are ordered from smallest to largest.
In addition, a boolean is also returned, which indicates whether this
unit is favorable (i.e. not an outdated or otherwise undesirable unit).
Internal class for representing a unit.

classes.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.progress
documentation:
How far the progress bar is filled. A value of 0 means that the bar is
empty, while a value of `bar.max` means that the bar is completely
filled.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.max
documentation: The value at which the bar is considered completely filled.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.width
documentation: The progress bar's width, in characters.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.fraction
documentation: The bar's current progress fraction, as float in range [0, 1].

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.unit
documentation: The unit used to display the progress bar's value.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.complete
documentation:
Convenience method which sets the bar's progress to its maximum value
and undocks the bar if it is docked.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.eta
documentation:
The estimated time until the progress bar is complete, or `None` if
there is not enough data to calculate an ETA with reasonable confidence.

methods.name: revel.legacy.ProgressBar.bar_string
documentation:
The string representation of the progress bar. This only includes the
bar, not any text around it. The returned string may be colored, and
thus should be printed as markup.

A progress bar, duh.

Sample usage:

```py
bar = print(
    ProgressBar(max=100, unit="count"),
    dock=True,
)

for ii in range(100):
    bar.progress = ii
    time.sleep(0.1)

bar.complete()
```
